author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Defining components 'in the spec repo at the commit' is unclear; readers of the spec shouldn't need to consult the README or package.json to determine what the spec is referring to.
principle_hint: spec should be self-contained, avoid external indirection

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Questions why a removed/replaced spec point was moved from its previous position rather than kept alongside its replacement.
principle_hint: keep replaced spec points adjacent to replacements

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggests defining the protocol version value by referencing another spec point rather than hard-coding it, so it can be updated in one place.
principle_hint: reference values indirectly through other spec points

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Replaced spec points should be ordered alongside the thing they've been replaced by so the sequence makes coherent sense.
principle_hint: order spec points for coherent reading

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Objects to putting the protocol version string in package.json; effectively annexes metadata to be part of the spec. Argues that a protocol version change is a genuine behavioural change that should follow spec point replacement, not be hidden via indirection.
principle_hint: don't hide behavioural changes via metadata indirection

pr_title: Clarify the circumstances under which RTP8f applies
author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Notes that a clarifying behavioural change should be a new spec point rather than modifying an existing one.
principle_hint: behavioural changes need new spec points

pr_title: Define API for accessing Agent identifier and its components
author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Clarifies that a new ClientLibraryInfo class provides library-level information without instantiating a client, distinct from per-instance ClientOptions.agents. Responds to concern about needing runtime info by suggesting a method instead of a property if needed.
principle_hint: distinguish library-level from instance-level APIs

pr_title: Define API for accessing Agent identifier and its components
author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Ties method existence conditionally to another API: 'The client library must offer this method if and only if it offers the ClientOptions#agents property'.
principle_hint: link conditional API requirements explicitly

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Notes that adding a parenthetical cross-reference like '(redundant to RTN19a)' isn't strictly allowed by the Removal format in CONTRIBUTING.md; suggests either adjusting the contribution rules or following them.
principle_hint: follow documented conventions for removal text

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Points out that listing multiple spec points as replacements isn't covered by the CONTRIBUTING format for 'Replacement'; flags mismatch between contributing rules and usage.
principle_hint: contributing guide must cover real-world cases

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggests adding guidance to CONTRIBUTING.md about how to handle removal of a clause that has subclauses.
principle_hint: document edge cases in contributing guide

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggests retroactively applying newly added guidance (restoring subclauses marked as removed) for consistency with the new rule.
principle_hint: apply new conventions consistently across spec

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Questions the purpose of a Specification version number, arguing that feature spec compatibility could be expressed as the set of immutable feature spec points a library implements. Also raises that the release process is undocumented, making it unclear how a writer should know which version to cite when removing a feature.
principle_hint: justify versioning via concrete use cases

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Argues that 'Service version' is a misleading name since a given Ably service version supports multiple protocol versions, and 'protocol version' is more natural. Also questions putting protocol version in meta.yaml as metadata, since protocol changes are concrete changes that should use spec item replacement.
principle_hint: name concepts accurately; avoid overloaded terminology

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asks whether described behaviour is that of the REST API or behaviour that the SDK must implement; these should be clearly distinguished in the spec.
principle_hint: distinguish REST API behaviour from SDK behaviour

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggests making a class name more specific (BatchPublishSpec rather than the generic BatchSpec) since it's only used for publishes.
principle_hint: use specific, unambiguous type names

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asks for clarification on result structure (how many BatchPublishResult objects correspond to a BatchSpec for m messages and n channels) and whether users can associate results with input messages.
principle_hint: specify result shape and input-output mapping

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Flags inconsistency between type name in header (BatchPublishResult) and body text (BatchPublishChannelResult).
principle_hint: ensure naming consistency within a spec point

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggests aligning a type name with terminology already used in REST API documentation ('target specifier') to avoid using 'target' and 'target specifier' interchangeably.
principle_hint: align terminology with existing docs

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Suggests making a method overload (single-arg variant) optional because it doesn't translate cleanly to languages without overloading (e.g., Go).
principle_hint: accommodate idioms of diverse target languages

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Warns against SDK-side validation on a 'type' field beyond 'nonempty string', since Ably may add more types in future.
principle_hint: avoid client-side validation that blocks future values

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Suggests encoding stronger type guarantees (tagged union of two concrete shapes) rather than marking many fields optional, for languages that support it.
principle_hint: express type unions rather than optional fields

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Points out ambiguous wording: phrasing made the return overload seem optional while still mandating the argument overload. Proposes explicit rewording that marks the overload itself optional in idiomatic languages.
principle_hint: disambiguate optionality of overloads and arguments

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Requests explicit specification of how a 2D input (messages x channels) maps to a 1D result array — 'same order' is insufficient without defining the flattening.
principle_hint: specify flattening/ordering explicitly

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Argues against exposing internal (ProtocolMessage) identifiers in the public API without explanation; suggests renaming messageId to messageIdPrefix with accompanying documentation.
principle_hint: don't leak internal concepts into public API

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Notes that renaming the property in the batch API but not the (identical) normal publish response would be bad API coherence; breaking-change churn must be justified.
principle_hint: keep naming coherent across related APIs

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Questions whether a field is intentionally optional, and whether a qualifying phrase like 'if the request succeeded' in a sibling spec point is thereby redundant.
principle_hint: audit optionality and redundancy between subclauses

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Flags that IDL and prose disagree on field types (types of appliesAt and issuedBefore swapped between IDL and text).
principle_hint: keep IDL and prose consistent

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Notes that an error description should probably include the HTTP statusCode explicitly.
principle_hint: specify HTTP status codes alongside error codes

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Argues that documentation comments and sdk-api-reference PRs should be created alongside the spec PR, so reviewers can understand how users will interact with new functionality while reviewing.
principle_hint: pair spec changes with user-facing docs

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asks multiple clarification questions about a timestamp field's meaning, copy-paste errors in wording, and whether the SDK should convert raw values to Time objects.
principle_hint: clarify units, meaning, and idiomatic conversion

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Explains an id-shaped field isn't a Time and shouldn't be converted; distinguishes identifier-format fields from time-format ones. Notes that most users only need the 'set or not' semantic regardless of internal interpretation.
principle_hint: don't convert identifier-shaped values to Time

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Suggests including additional fields (schema, appId) in the response type because they are useful for interpreting other fields.
principle_hint: include fields needed to interpret other fields

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Questions making a feature 'optional', since all spec points are at SDKs' discretion anyway. Also notes 'may' is overloaded: once describing server behaviour, once describing optional SDK API choice, making it harder to read.
principle_hint: avoid overloading 'may'; minimise optionality

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Invokes an existing constraint (RTL2g: never emit a state change for same-state) to narrow available choices to either an UPDATE event or null, aligning with EventEmitter semantics.
principle_hint: reconcile new behaviour with existing invariants

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggests adding a cross-reference ('This attribute should be set as defined by RTL2i') to make clear that the SDK doesn't independently decide the value.
principle_hint: cross-reference to avoid duplicate requirements

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Recommends aligning wording to the 'optional callback' language used in RTL7c, and updating to match changed wording in the corresponding #attach spec point, for consistency.
principle_hint: align wording with sibling spec points

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Flags what looks like a differently-worded duplicate of another spec point (RTL4d1).
principle_hint: avoid differently-worded duplicate spec points

pr_title: refactor(RSC19f1): mention that protocol version is specified by CSV2
author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Notes an 'as specified by CSV2' reference is misleading because CSV2 doesn't define the concept of protocol version; suggests making any deviation from CSV2 explicit.
principle_hint: link to spec points that actually define the concept

pr_title: RFC: Distinguish the different meanings of a "message"
author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Argues against splitting publish and subscribe Message into two classes in the spec; many SDK languages don't represent nullability, so requiring two nearly identical classes would be redundant. Suggests IDL notes like 'optional for Message objects provided by the user' so each SDK can idiomatically choose. Also floats pulling the protocol page into the feature spec, since a clean split between public behaviour and wire protocol has failed in practice.
principle_hint: avoid spec constructs that don't map to target languages

pr_title: RFC: Distinguish the different meanings of a "message"
author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Argues the IDL does represent nullability, so the marker should be 'guaranteed to be populated for messages received from Ably'. Also argues that a separate protocol spec can still be valuable didactically, to help implementers understand the motivation behind the feature spec.
principle_hint: use IDL nullability; keep protocol rationale

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Flags mismatch between prose and IDL ('The callback is still named updateFailedCallback in the IDL').
principle_hint: keep IDL and prose in sync on renames

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Explains that changing an existing callback's type and behaviour is a breaking change that requires a major version bump and should target the integration branch. Suggests deprecating the old callback and adding a new optional one instead.
principle_hint: prefer additive deprecation over breaking changes

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Prefers an optional argument at the end of a signature for readability; also pushes for consistent optional-syntax in the IDL.
principle_hint: place optional args last; keep IDL syntax consistent

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Requests that a corresponding PR be opened in sdk-api-reference, signalling that spec changes should propagate to the API reference.
principle_hint: pair spec changes with sdk-api-reference updates

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Warns that phrasing 'discarding related changes' wrongly implies the SDK must remember and revert to the pre-sync presence set; this isn't necessary.
principle_hint: avoid implying extra state not actually required

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Objects to adding a 'during sync' qualifier because sync just surfaces changes that may have happened at any time during suspension; the wording misrepresents when the events happened.
principle_hint: don't conflate event timing with sync timing

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Notes a new addition is unclear ('conditions side effects', 'while enter presence message').
principle_hint: keep prose grammatical and precise

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Objects to 'A test should exist that...' spec items: the spec should specify behaviour and let implementers decide tests. Spec isn't exhaustive about tests anyway.
principle_hint: spec should describe behaviour, not prescribe tests

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Suggests a clearer section heading like 'Effect of channel state on enter(), update(), and leave() methods' rather than the vaguer 'enter, update, and leave presence messages'.
principle_hint: make section titles specific and unambiguous

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Explains that a new sub-item added in the middle of a parent whose children each list one outcome of a disjunction breaks the structure; a shared requirement across siblings shouldn't be a sibling itself.
principle_hint: respect parent-child semantics of subclauses

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Pushes back on a tense change (past → present): the original phrasing said 'don't change it in this case', which is lost in the rewrite.
principle_hint: preserve meaning when rewording

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Warns against defining terms ad hoc; 'transport' is OSI terminology (e.g., a websocket), not a connection; RTN19a applies to any new transport, not just a new connectionId.
principle_hint: use precise, established terminology

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Notes a spec item is obsolete due to other items (RTN15c6/7) handling attach, and that the detach portion is no longer needed; recommends trimming to just the still-relevant behaviour.
principle_hint: remove redundancy as spec evolves

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Insists on DRY: referring back to the spec item defining a condition rather than repeating it with slightly different wording, so requirements can't drift.
principle_hint: don't repeat conditions; reference defining spec point

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Suggests adding an explanatory sentence to the spec because current wording is unclear about how the library can detect whether recover succeeded without a connectionId to compare.
principle_hint: add explanatory sentences where implementation is ambiguous

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Suggests the spec could explicitly enumerate which properties are part of 'connection state' that should be cleared, for clarity.
principle_hint: enumerate state properties rather than leaving implicit

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Argues that a spec point waffling on the rationale should be pared down to just the normative requirement; rationale-only digressions dilute the spec.
principle_hint: keep spec points normative, drop rationale

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Notes that a protocol version bump that changes a response shape is a breaking API change for users of rest.request() and should be paired with a library major version bump per semver.
principle_hint: align protocol bumps with semver-major library releases

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Raises that bumping protocol should be accompanied by removing stale related requirements (e.g., the newBatchResponse query param mandate).
principle_hint: clean up obsolete requirements when bumping

pr_title: Spec on what should happen when the recover option is malformed.
author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggests wording: 'If the recovery key ... cannot be deserialized due to malformed data, then an error should be logged and the connection should be made like no recover option was provided.'
principle_hint: specify explicit fallback behaviour

pr_title: Spec on what should happen when the recover option is malformed.
author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Agrees with simple spec but notes a richer behaviour (e.g., adding an error to the next connected state change event) would be better ergonomically; rejected due to implementation cost across SDKs.
principle_hint: weigh ergonomics vs cross-SDK implementation cost

pr_title: Updated the spec so that it reflects the behaviour implemented in ably-cocoa PR #1847
author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Points out that the proposed change only partially describes the actual SDK behaviour (other spec points would also need changing) and that spec point modification rules must be followed.
principle_hint: match spec fully to behaviour; follow modification rules

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asserts the wording change doesn't justify the referenced implementation commit and that spec points can't be silently edited; must follow the Modification guidelines.
principle_hint: don't silently edit spec points

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Argues 'moves into' is ambiguous; people interpret it differently. Either clarify the spec point precisely or accept that a parenthetical that seems to refine behaviour needs rewording.
principle_hint: eliminate ambiguous state-transition language

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Agrees wording became ambiguous; suggests simplifying by removing channel state references and keying behaviour off receipt of an ATTACHED ProtocolMessage.
principle_hint: prefer wire-event triggers over derived state triggers

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Points out a proposed wording introduces a contradiction ('whenever' + exception) and the current proposal is itself a behaviour change from the existing spec; asks for non-contradictory phrasing.
principle_hint: avoid self-contradictory wording

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Proposes non-contradictory wording: 'should perform automatic re-entry whenever the channel receives an ATTACHED ProtocolMessage, except in the case where the channel is already attached and the ProtocolMessage has the RESUMED bit flag set.'
principle_hint: state exceptions with 'except' rather than separate sentences

pr_title: Added forgotten test case for RTL2f
author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Reiterates dislike of 'A test should exist...' spec items; spec should specify behaviour and leave test design to implementers.
principle_hint: spec should describe behaviour, not prescribe tests

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Suggests listing concrete states (INITIALIZED, DETACHING, DETACHED) for when implicit attach applies, since it doesn't make sense for already-attached/attaching/suspended channels.
principle_hint: enumerate applicable states explicitly

pr_title: TO3g editing in place for clarity.
author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Rejects inventing terms ('publish message' as a noun is not used elsewhere), flags a contradictory 'If x, y, otherwise z' structure whose logic is inverted, and notes each sibling clause states a slightly different condition, making the overall requirement ambiguous.
principle_hint: use consistent terminology; avoid contradictory conditionals

pr_title: RSA7f: clarify that send clientId regardless of basic/token auth
author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Decided to edit RSA7e in place rather than adding RSA7f and deprecating the old one, arguing that since no SDK actually implemented the old RSA7e as written, the spec should just reflect the implemented reality rather than pretend older versions were valid.
principle_hint: align spec with implemented reality rather than historical fiction

pr_title: RSA7f: clarify that send clientId regardless of basic/token auth
author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Objected to duplicating clientId validity restrictions on DRY grounds; RSA7c is the single place where a valid clientId is defined, so any additional restrictions should be added there.
principle_hint: keep a single canonical spec point per definition (DRY)

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Noted that mapping a boolean client option (echoMessages) to a string connection parameter (echo="true"/"false") is not obvious and should be specified explicitly.
principle_hint: specify explicit type conversions, don't rely on obviousness

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Deliberately implementing the spec in Swift without consulting the JS implementation in order to surface ambiguities and ensure the spec is self-sufficient.
principle_hint: test spec sufficiency via implementation in isolation

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked that spec points not be renamed once SDKs have begun referring to them; reverted a rename commit.
principle_hint: avoid renaming spec points once implementations reference them

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Restored deleted spec point IDs and pointed contributors at CONTRIBUTING.md guidance on modifying the spec; argued that now that the spec has SDK implementations, the modification rules must be followed strictly.
principle_hint: follow CONTRIBUTING.md rules for spec point modification

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggested highlighting when statements are non-normative summaries whose meaning will be fleshed out by later spec points, so readers don't try to implement vague prose as-is.
principle_hint: distinguish non-normative summary text from normative requirements

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether it was a conscious choice not to describe the SDK API in terms of signatures/types and IDL, as the core features spec does.
principle_hint: prefer describing APIs via signatures/types/IDL

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggested replacing the word ATTACHED in a prose description of the ATTACH operation, because ATTACHED is also the name of a state and the overlap is confusing.
principle_hint: avoid reusing state names for operation names

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked that error specifications be explicit about which type is used (e.g. ErrorInfo) rather than relying on implicit knowledge from other SDKs.
principle_hint: make implicit type references explicit

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: If ErrorInfo is reused everywhere, the spec should specify statusCode in all places it describes throwing such an error, not only code.
principle_hint: specify both code and statusCode for every thrown error

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked for clarification of the word "in sequence": does it mean awaiting each attach() call's success before starting the next?
principle_hint: define ambiguous procedural adverbs like 'in sequence'

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether success means the attach() call completed successfully or some observable state was reached; the reader shouldn't have to guess whether to monitor channel state.
principle_hint: distinguish call-return success from state-observation

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked for a cross-reference to the spec point that describes the behaviour referred to here, so the reader doesn't have to search.
principle_hint: add spec-point cross-references for referenced behaviour

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked that when a term like "must be rolled back" is used, the spec should signal that a subsequent spec point will define what it means.
principle_hint: flag forward references to later spec points

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether "fails to attach" is defined as "the attach() call threw an error".
principle_hint: define failure conditions precisely

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Requested that information be inlined rather than referring the reader to another SDK's implementation, citing prior cleanup of "see Ruby for more info" in the main spec.
principle_hint: spec should be self-contained, not reference SDKs

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggested expanding a terse rule into explicit steps (when channel.attach() fails, inspect state, then transition using errorReason as the cause) and asking what code/statusCode that transition's error should carry.
principle_hint: spell out procedural steps and error fields

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked how a room ATTACH operation should end when a channel entered FAILED, including timing relative to room state transitions.
principle_hint: specify completion semantics and ordering for operations

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggested moving a spec point about RETRY into the same "Room Lifecycle Operations" section alongside ATTACH/DETACH/RELEASE, for consistency with an earlier enumeration.
principle_hint: group related spec points structurally

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked what "enter the recovery loop" refers to, and how one operation initiating another interacts with operations that are already queued.
principle_hint: specify inter-operation interaction when one triggers another

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether "When the room enters the SUSPENDED status" means whenever or only in the context of a specific triggering point.
principle_hint: qualify 'when X happens' triggers by scope

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether a thrown error refers to a specific ErrorInfo code rather than an unspecified error.
principle_hint: specify exact error codes, not generic errors

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether the rule is scoped to "the same room ID" or applies universally.
principle_hint: disambiguate scope (per-entity vs global)

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggested expressing sequencing in terms of a queue of operations picked off one-by-one, because "asynchronously" is too vague.
principle_hint: model concurrency via explicit queue semantics

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked that status codes be specified everywhere an error code is specified, including the Chat-specific Error Codes section and any in-text numeric error codes.
principle_hint: always specify statusCode alongside error code

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked that the spec explicitly reference contributor.errorReason rather than use the ambiguous phrase "the Realtime Channel error".
principle_hint: name the exact property when referring to errors

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether a subpoint describes post-operation behaviour, i.e. whether side-effects happen outside the context of the lifecycle manager operation itself.
principle_hint: clarify whether effects are in-operation or after

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked why an error differs from the one used in a closely related state transition, suggesting consistency or an explicit justification is needed.
principle_hint: justify divergent error choices between related points

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Requested explicit code and statusCode for an error that is mentioned without those fields.
principle_hint: every error needs code and statusCode

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggested that spec points that throw errors should refer to entries in the "Chat-specific Error Codes" section rather than declaring codes inline.
principle_hint: centralise error definitions and reference them

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Noted that clarifications applied to ATTACH should apply to DETACH, which has parallel language; parallel spec points need parallel treatment.
principle_hint: apply clarifications consistently across symmetric points

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Noted ambiguity when a spec point describes two errors and later text refers to "the error from CHA-RL1h2" without saying which.
principle_hint: when a point has multiple errors, disambiguate references

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Flagged a sentence that seems to duplicate or contradict earlier language about how the cause field is populated, suggesting it might be a leftover.
principle_hint: remove stale/duplicate prose from spec points

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Found duplicate spec point IDs (e.g. CHA-RL3c) and asked for the find-duplicate-spec-items CI script to be updated to cover Chat, providing a ready implementation.
principle_hint: automate detection of duplicate spec-point IDs in CI

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Requested that DETACH spec points inherit the same clarifying language that was added to ATTACH (e.g. defining "fails to detach" in terms of the call throwing an error).
principle_hint: mirror clarifying language across symmetric operations

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether the spec really requires polling for channel state DETACHED, or merely means to wait until detach() returned or the channel became FAILED.
principle_hint: prefer call-return semantics over state polling

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether "enters another state" is an event-listener thing or merely means reading the channel state after detach() fails.
principle_hint: clarify whether to subscribe or read state

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether a general failure spec point also applies to the retry case introduced in the same section.
principle_hint: clarify scope of rules across retries

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked for an alternative to the undefined term "detachment cycle".
principle_hint: avoid introducing undefined domain terms

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked why the duration of a "short pause" is left unspecified.
principle_hint: specify concrete durations for waits/retries

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Same question about an unspecified "short wait" duration.
principle_hint: specify concrete durations for waits/retries

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked for an alternative to the undefined term "channel detach sequence".
principle_hint: avoid undefined domain terms

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Requested the same clarification of "fails to detach" (i.e. that detach() threw) applied elsewhere.
principle_hint: define failure in terms of call return/throw

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether retry is indefinite (until detach succeeds or FAILED) rather than a single attempt.
principle_hint: specify retry termination conditions

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether a given rule also applies to retry attempts described elsewhere.
principle_hint: clarify rule applicability under retries

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether "the detach operation" refers to the individual channel detach or the outer room detach operation.
principle_hint: distinguish channel-level vs room-level operations

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether "once all channels have entered DETACHED" really means we check state, or rather that detach() returned successfully on each non-FAILED channel; raised contradiction concerns.
principle_hint: prefer call-completion to state-observation, avoid internal contradictions

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked for explicit ordering of checks around a wait: does the FAILED-check happen before the wait, after, or both?
principle_hint: specify exact order of steps in retry loops

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether a spec point adds any new requirement beyond what neighbouring spec points already say, raising redundancy concerns.
principle_hint: flag potentially redundant spec points

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked the author to enumerate the full set of possible channel states at this point so the spec is exhaustive.
principle_hint: enumerate all possible states at a decision point

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Corrected a field name (resumed not resume) used in several spec points.
principle_hint: match exact field names in prose

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Said a spec point was too vague to implement as written; asked for clarification of whether "attached" means the initial call succeeded or the channel eventually reached ATTACHED.
principle_hint: prefer unambiguous, implementable phrasing

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asked whether a condition is merely a reworded negation of a prior condition, suggesting duplication should be unified.
principle_hint: unify duplicated conditions across spec points

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggested consolidating a convoluted conditional into a single clear spec point that states the timeout, the trigger, and the room state transition explicitly.
principle_hint: collapse convoluted nested conditions into clear statements

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Flagged that "channel lifecycle operation" is probably used where "room lifecycle operation" is meant.
principle_hint: use the correct layer (room vs channel) consistently

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Flags that an error being removed is still referred to elsewhere in the spec.

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Points out that a tombstone marker for a removed spec point was accidentally dropped in the edit.

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asks whether it's intentional that a fixed 250ms wait still applies even when detach() caused the channel to enter FAILED, because that behaviour may be surprising.

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Observes that a flag appears to only ever be set to false and never flipped to true, suggesting it is vestigial or mis-specified.

author: lawrence-forooghian

principle_hint: optimise current readability over future-proofing wording

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: SimonWoolf

principle_hint: keep test/infrastructure fixtures out of features spec

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Questions whether renaming an enum case is a breaking change for users relying on the old name.
principle_hint: treat enum renames as breaking changes

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asks whether a subtle change included in this PR is actually a latent bug fix affecting existing users, suggesting it should be split into its own PR.
principle_hint: isolate bug fixes from feature PRs

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Suggests rewording so the text distinguishes between the parameter name 'messageSerial' and what it semantically represents (the Message's serial), to make downstream spec points intelligible.

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asks whether the asymmetry — client-side size check for message publishes but not for annotation publishes/deletes — is intentional, and likewise for realtime.

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asks what statusCode accompanies the error code cited in the spec point.

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

principle_hint: update global ACK/protocol lists for new message types

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Questions whether the referenced aggregation-types documentation is in scope for this PR and whether an existing docs URL could be linked.
principle_hint: link to external docs only when they exist

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asks for explanation of what the map keys are and how they relate to Annotation.type.

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: SimonWoolf

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: SimonWoolf

author: SimonWoolf

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Argues that throwing an error for bad channel modes fits neither programmer-error nor runtime-error categories in Swift; prefers that the spec allow a warn log as an alternative, so SDKs can adopt what's idiomatic for their language.
principle_hint: allow language-idiomatic error vs. warning choice

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Accepts making the subscribe error discretionary; reflects on how JS's async functions collapse programmer and runtime errors into promise rejection, which is sometimes worse for API clarity.

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Follows up asking whether the PR that was supposed to pull out an unrelated change was ever opened.
principle_hint: verify split-out PRs actually land

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Asks author to update the docstrings and IDL to match the newly-agreed spec wording.

principle_hint: honour prior ADR/RTF decisions absent strong justification

author: SimonWoolf

author: SimonWoolf

author: SimonWoolf

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

author: lawrence-forooghian

excerpt: Argues that none of the new spec items belong in the SDK spec because they instruct the server, not the SDK; it's not the SDK's job to interpret, validate, or act on params — RTL4k already specifies the SDK must encode and send them.
principle_hint: spec is for SDK behaviour, not server behaviour

pr_title: feat: add channel-level echo suppression spec points
author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Distinguishes the SDK instruction (turn echoMessages into an echo querystring param) from explanatory docstring-style content; the latter is user-facing documentation, not actionable for an SDK dev, and shouldn't be in the spec.
principle_hint: separate SDK requirements from user documentation

pr_title: feat: add channel-level echo suppression spec points
author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: User-visible docstrings shouldn't be wordy or try to enumerate params (the linked user docs already have the list), and shouldn't reference a spec item identifier, which is meaningless to users integrating the library.
principle_hint: user docstrings shouldn't reference spec item ids

pr_title: feat: add channel-level echo suppression spec points
author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Agrees with Simon that end-to-end behaviour spec points are actively confusing because they invite agents to implement client logic that shouldn't exist (cites RTP9b presence where an agent wrote a local-check-then-ENTER path). Supports test-assertion spec points as a cross-SDK way to enforce end-to-end behaviour when no better mechanism exists.
principle_hint: don't spec client logic for server behaviour

pr_title: feat: add channel-level echo suppression spec points
author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Rejects the notion that RTL4j2 is a precedent for speccing out all server behaviour — only one channelparams test is needed to verify encoding. Argues SDK test suites shouldn't aim to be comprehensive server-behaviour tests (that's realtime repo's job) and that merging SDK spec with user docs would cause scope bloat and incoherence; better to keep the spec focused and cross-reference docs.
principle_hint: keep SDK spec focused; use realtime repo for server tests

author: lawrence-forooghian
excerpt: Agrees spec items should be one per line without wrapping since no autoformatter will maintain wrapping during edits; also notes tools like git show --word-diff play poorly with wrapped lines.
principle_hint: one spec item per line; no line wrapping

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Opposes the decision to wrap lines in the Markdown migration; one line per spec item is easier for LLMs and gives cleaner diffs. If wrapping is used, an autoformatter and max_line_length in editorconfig are required.
principle_hint: one spec item per line; add autoformatter if wrapping

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Questions whether the use case justifies the spec change (why would bringing React components in/out of scope need release?) and notes frustration at discovering an earlier decision to make release() implicitly detach was never implemented in ably-js.
principle_hint: ensure spec decisions are actually implemented

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Concedes the point and suggests removing the required-channel-state bit and making ably-js detach on release, but argues the docstring should still warn people off release() since it was always an advanced-user API without guardrails.
principle_hint: flag 'advanced/unsafe' APIs in docstrings

author: SimonWoolf
excerpt: Would prefer release() surface an error rather than be automagic, but accepts cross-SDK uniformity matters more, since changing other SDKs to ably-js semantics would be a breaking change.
principle_hint: cross-SDK consistency outweighs individual API aesthetics
